ref: The next morning the king was upset. So he called in his magicians and wise men and told them what he had dreamed. None of them could tell him what the dreams meant.
text: Na ɔtanta ɔmmɔ ni, kasusu kékpoku Ƒarao geen. Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ɔ́ya na bálɔ bekebone ku Egyipteƒuɔ banundane béte-nwu. Na ɔ́sɔku ana ani na úgye ƒui ɔ́su-ma. Nsu ɔdi útakana na udie-nya kalɔ ute-nwu.
translated: And the people multiplied and became numerous, and the land was filled with them. The Egyptians became fearful of them and put heavy burdens on them with hard labor. And they built storage cities for Pharaoh, Pithom and Rameses. And the more they afflicted them, the more they multiplied and spread out.

text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Sodom bekyuli bebo kuli ɔkplu na bátaabla alaekpidi lidu lidu taaƒiɔ Katɔti.
translated: And the men of the city, the men of Sodom, surrounded the house, both young and old, all the people from every quarter of Kato.

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Anwuɔsu uwi ɔni na Yakob ɔ́si kemo úbeduo useyi ɔmmɔ ni, Lea útásiaku-nwu ulu na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ɔdi ka abatɛ-mi kudɔ ɔmwi. Ɔsika lɛ́ƒɔ-ɔ líse ɔkpa na kɔwɔ ligyiitu lɛni na Rahel ɔ́ƒɔ mina ubi kudɔ ɔsu.” Anwuɔsu úsie útátɛ Lea kudɔ usien ɔmmɔ.
translated: And Jacob did so and completed the week of this one, and Leah's sister Rachel he took to be his wife also, and he served Laban another seven years for Rachel. And Laban gave his daughter Rachel his servant Bilhah to be her servant. And Jacob went in to Rachel also, and he loved Rachel more than Leah.

ref: I will give you as many descendants as there are stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of this land. They will be a blessing to every nation on earth,
text: Na ƒula bawɔ ni, ndubɔya na bemomo aani ayabi ani na aanu na kayaa. Na ɔkɛ ƒula kaba kemmo na ndubute kayawi ƒui bekyuli kusɛ kusɛ.
translated: The tortoise and the rabbit, the elephant and the monkey and the dog and the cat and the parrot, they all went to the forest to look for food to eat together.

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Amu na ɔnana ɔmmɔ ɔ́su Yakob ɔnini, “Asɔ aladi lɛnyi anii Yakob. Mmomu bekyuli badubɔlɔ-ɔ banii Israel, ɔsika énye asiaku Unwoti Katɔti ku bekyuli na ádi lɛlɔ.”
translated: And his mother said to Jacob, "Your brother Esau is coming. He has four hundred men with him, and he is planning to kill you. Now, my son, do what I tell you."

ref: Jacob went to his father Isaac at Hebron, also called Mamre or Kiriath-Arba, where Isaac's father Abraham had lived as a foreigner.
text: Na Yakob ulu busie kemmo ni, úbedu ɔti kudɔ na bana ɔma Mamre ɔni na ude Kiriat Arba ɔni basɔ baalɔ banii Hebron. Mmɔ na Abraham ku Isak bélie dii.
translated: And Jacob came to his father Isaac at Mamre, or Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac had dwelled.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.

translated: The king of the land was very wicked, and Pharaoh was very angry. The people were suffering and crying, and they were looking for someone to save them.
